dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson c †Buttler b Rabada 11 (15b 2x4 0x6 16m) SR: 73.33

dismissalComment.html: Samson beaten by jaffas from Siraj, nicked by a shorty widey ball from Rabada. More length but with that width, it's made for the cut. 148ks, Samson reaches out for the cut with the upper body and no footwork, wanted to go square. Got a thin edge off the bottom of the bat that flew to Buttler's right

dismissalText.commentary: Urvil Patel c Holder b Rabada 4 (3b 1x4 0x6 2m) SR: 133.33

dismissalComment.html: Urvil's aggression lasts two balls, Rabada too hostile and accurate. He goes hard length at 149ks after being driven off a full length. Same line - around leg stump. Urvil made room but not enough, cramped on the flat-bat hit down the ground. Top-edge goes to mid-on's left

dismissalText.commentary: Sarfaraz Khan c †Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: Golden duck for the impact sub. Sarfaraz wants to pull his first ball over square leg for six. But it's a cross seamer that skids through just over off. Bat went from slightly low to high. Like Tendulkar vs McGrath in the 2003 final. This one though, went back to the keeper

dismissalText.commentary: Dewald Brevis c Shubman Gill b Suthar 2 (9b 0x4 0x6 17m) SR: 22.22

dismissalComment.html: No-look out! Gill takes the high ball at cover, well in the ring too. Pace in the early 80ks, fullish ball lands on middle and turns away. That's what did Brevis in. He made room and went for a swipe over long-on, diagonal-bat swipe. Bounce on the ball meant it took the top edge and went in the other direction.

dismissalText.commentary: Jamie Overton c Shahrukh Khan b Arshad Khan 18 (6b 3x4 1x6 10m) SR: 300

dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson c †Buttler b Rabada 11 (15b 2x4 0x6 16m) SR: 73.33

dismissalComment.html: Samson beaten by jaffas from Siraj, nicked by a shorty widey ball from Rabada. More length but with that width, it's made for the cut. 148ks, Samson reaches out for the cut with the upper body and no footwork, wanted to go square. Got a thin edge off the bottom of the bat that flew to Buttler's right

dismissalComment.html: No-look out! Gill takes the high ball at cover, well in the ring too. Pace in the early 80ks, fullish ball lands on middle and turns away. That's what did Brevis in. dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill st †Samson b Noor Ahmad 33 (23b 1x4 3x6 33m) SR: 143.47

dismissalComment.html: Appeal for stumping, sent up to third ump. 98ks length ball landed just down leg and went past the flick. Gill's back foot slid just outside the crease as Samson collected the ball and pushed the bails off his right hand. He was sliding leg side, didn't let the momentum carry his hands back. Gill's foot is centimetres in the air, if at all that, and that's enough for the third umpire to rule him out quickly

dismissalText.commentary: Shubman Gill st †Samson b Noor Ahmad 33 (23b 1x4 3x6 33m) SR: 143.47

dismissalComment.html: Appeal for stumping, sent up to third ump. 98ks length ball landed just down leg and went past the flick. Gill's back foot slid just outside the crease as Samson collected the ball and pushed the bails off his right hand. He was sliding leg side, didn't let the momentum carry his hands back. Gill's foot is centimetres in the air, if at all that, and that's enough for the third umpire to rule him out quickly

dismissalText.commentary: Sai Sudharsan c Brevis b Hosein 87 (46b 4x4 7x6 88m) SR: 189.13

dismissalComment.html: A little too late, but Brevis has taken a stunner to see the back of Sai Sudharsan. Dragged down outside off, he absolutely shellacks the pull to the right of Brevis at deep midwicket, who runs to his right, flings out both hands and takes an excellent diving catch.
